essence: Investigates noise complaints, measures sound levels, and enforces regulations to protect community wellbeing.

identity.title: Noise Control Officer
identity.summary: Assesses noise from homes, businesses, construction, and industry; uses sound‑monitoring equipment; enforces noise regulations; and helps reduce environmental noise impacts.

salary.notes: Specialists in industrial noise, environmental permitting, or acoustic investigations can exceed £55k-£70k+.

qualifications: Training in acoustics, environmental health, or environmental science
Knowledge of noise regulations and environmental‑protection law
Ability to use sound‑level meters and acoustic‑monitoring equipment
Understanding of noise impacts, mitigation, and enforcement procedures

lifestyle.description: Noise control officers work for councils, environmental‑health teams, and regulatory agencies. They investigate noise complaints, measure sound levels, assess nuisance, and enforce noise regulations. The role suits individuals who enjoy investigation, fieldwork, and protecting community wellbeing.

habits: Investigates noise complaints from residents and businesses
Measures sound levels using acoustic equipment
Assesses noise nuisance and regulatory breaches
Advises on noise mitigation and compliance
Prepares reports and supports enforcement actions
